mission.title: cartography: AWS Sync: Try all AWS accounts, and raise Exception afterwards
mission.description: AWS Sync: Try all AWS accounts, and raise Exception afterwards. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cartography deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cartography version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cartography -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cartography
helm list -n cartography 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cartography installation.

steps.title: Review cartography configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cartography configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cartography -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cartography
kubectl get configmap -n cartography -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cartography
```
*Title*: *Try all AWS accounts, and raise Exception afterwards*

*Description*:
Sometimes a single AWS account's sync could fail. In such situations, we more likely want to proceed with the rest of our accounts, but still be notified of the failure.

steps.title: Apply the fix for AWS Sync: Try all AWS accounts, and raise Exception…
steps.description:
How do we feel about enabling this as an optional cli switch?

   I think it's okay to run the cleanup jobs, so long as all the cleanup jobs are account-specific. Most of them are, except for a few statements:
*
```yaml
failed_accont_ids = []
for account_id in account_ids:
    try:
        _sync_one_account(account_id)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception(e)
        failed_accont_ids.append(account_id)
if failed_accont_ids:
    raise Exception(f'Syncs failed for accounts {failed_accont_ids}')
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cartography -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cartography
kubectl get events -n cartography --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "AWS Sync: Try all AWS accounts, and raise Exception…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
How do we feel about enabling this as an optional cli switch?

   I think it's okay to run the cleanup jobs, so long as all the cleanup jobs are account-specific.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cartography installed or the issue environment reproducible.
